title: Persons and Pronouns
description: Build a stable base for Bulgarian grammar by learning the six person forms, the main pronouns, and the rule that pronouns are often left out when the verb already shows the subject.

content.title: The six person forms
content.description: Bulgarian verbs normally tell you who is acting, so the person is visible in the ending even when the pronoun disappears.

rows: gender only matters in the 3rd person singular

content.title: Drop the pronoun when you can
content.text: Аз чета and Чета both mean I read / I am reading. The verb ending usually gives enough information, so Bulgarian often sounds more natural without the pronoun.

content.title: Pronoun set
content.description: These are the most common subject pronouns you will see in everyday Bulgarian.

content.title: Watch-outs
items: Вие can mean more than one person or a polite singular you.
Той, тя, and то all map to the third person singular but differ by gender.
The pronoun is optional when the verb form is already clear.

content.title: Person in action
content.description: These examples show the same verb form with and without the pronoun.

content.note: The pronoun is explicit here for emphasis.
Natural everyday version. The ending already identifies the person.
Plural person uses the -т ending in this present-tense pattern.

content.question: Which Bulgarian pronoun can be singular polite or plural?
content.note: Check whether the sentence is formal or plural from the context.
